title: quercus sessilis var. sublobata
intro: Quercus sessilis var. sublobata, also known as the sublobate sessile oak or sessile oak, is a variety of evergreen tree in the beech family that is native to East Asia. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Quercus sessilis var. sublobata are believed to include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a digestive aid. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in traditional East Asian medicine, often in the form of teas or infusions made from its leaves or bark.
